reviews.review_msg: If you seen his name on your enrollment list by goSFU, just choose it, you will never regret your decision! All the best
I was really enjoyed taking his class, he is an expert for the sustainability area prof! Have fun guys
He taught this class very well, compared to the other faculty, he is the best
He is really a good prof, excellent

Kam has so much meaningful knowledge to share and he really cares about his students. We explored our role in sustainable business and how to approach global issues. Class content is very manageable if you show up to class and do the pre-work. Super recommend Kam.
All around awesome guy and he truly cares about your learning.

He's a great lecturer. He's well-spoken, interactive, and clear during lectures. Attendance gets recorded in tutorials. He emphasizes participation and takes record of whether you contributed to lecture discussions.
Really good professor, knows what he is talking about.

His course is very structured and you know what to expect. His lectures are suitable for any class level & size and his way of speaking was neither too intensive or unengaging. From what I know, he previously taught higher-level courses and electives but I see he's more than capable of handling required lower-division courses, which is not easy.

Kam is great. As the creator of 275, he did a great job, and he does a great job teaching it. You can tell he is very passionate about the material. The course is very structured, and you know what to expect from assignments/quizzes/exams. He also provides a lot of help, as do the TA's. To excel, understand/memorize the concepts, and read.
Awesome prof especially for teaching this class for the first time. Good content as is but makes class very engaging and listens to student feedback.
Kam was very supportive, and you could tell he really cared about his students. The class I took with him didn't have any exams, it was mainly personal reflection papers and a group project.
